sections.name: OPENING
sections.content:
It's been a few months since you picked up a pair from us.

You had a reason to step back, and you were right to trust that.

We're not here to ask why you stopped using them, or to encourage you to try again. That's not our place. You know your child best. You know what's right for your family.

But we did want to let you know: we're still here.

---

sections.name: HOOK — Gentle Re-engagement
sections.content:
The underwear your child wore was designed around one idea: the body learns best when it gets the right signal.

For some children, that signal arrives quickly. For others, it takes time. For some, it looks different than expected, and that's still progress.

If your child paused. Looked down. Walked toward the bathroom, even after. Stayed dry a little longer than before.

Those moments matter. Not because they mean "it worked." Because they mean something shifted. Even a small shift is real.

---

sections.name: VALIDATION — Honor Their Choice
sections.content:
You've been doing this for years. You've seen products come and go. You've heard promises that didn't deliver.

If you put the underwear aside, that decision was valid. The product will be here. The 60-day guarantee will be here. So will I.

There is no deadline. No threshold you need to meet.

---

sections.name: MECHANISM REMINDER — The Signal, Gently Reframed
sections.content:
The body-signal underwear works by giving the brain a sensation it may never have received before, a gentle, sustained "uh-oh" feeling that lasts 30 to 60 seconds after the moment passes.

Pull-ups wick that sensation away. Regular underwear delivers it in a split second that the brain can't always catch. The BSL layer holds the signal long enough for a deeply nervous system that picks up signals slowly to register: *something happened.*

It doesn't force the brain to respond differently. It gives the brain the information it needs to make its own connections, in its own time.

---

sections.name: OFFER — 1 Pair ($34.99), Product After Mechanism
sections.content:
If you still have the pair you bought, you already have the mechanism. Pull it out. Let your child wear it alongside their current routine, during a quiet afternoon at home, without pressure, without a timer.

If you don't still have it, one pair is $34.99. Add it next to what you're already using. No replacement. No switching.

---

sections.name: CTA — Permission-Framed
sections.content:
You decide. No timeline. No push. If it fits your child's journey, it's here. If today's not the day, that's a real answer.

---

Next time: What parents who tried again discovered — and why the second attempt often looks different.

Lena Sign-Off (Winback):

> Whatever you choose, we're glad you were part of this community. The door is always open.
>
> With care,
>
> Lena
>
> *Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons*
>
> P.S. If you have a question about anything, how it works, what signs to look for, whether it's worth trying again, hit reply. You'll get me, not a template. I read everything.

---

### Pre-Send QA Checklist

| Check | Status |
|-------|--------|
| ZERO urgency indicators | ✅ |
| Dignity-first throughout | ✅ |
| Product after mechanism | ✅ (mechanism fully explained before mention) |
| 1pr $34.99 only | ✅ |
| No math reframe | ✅ |
| No "fix" / "cure" / "solve" | ✅ |
| No exclamation marks | ✅ |
| No cheerleader language | ✅ |
| Permission-framed CTA | ✅ |
| Grief NOT used as sales tool | ✅ |
| Lapse acknowledged without guilt | ✅ |
| Lena sign-off correct variant ("With care,") | ✅ |
| Reply-to Lena (support@brightkidco.com) | ✅ |

---

# PART 2 — CREATIVE STRATEGY

sections.name: 2.2 Imagery Direction
sections.content:
L3-Specific Image Rules:

- Hero image: None on E1. Use after the mechanism section in later emails if at all.
- If imagery is used: Photograph of a real mother (not a model, not studio-lit) sitting on a couch in soft natural light. Child not required in frame. Focus on the mother's expression, quiet, reflective, not smiling.
- Product shot: Flat-lay only. Underwear folded, on a neutral surface (wood, linen, cream). NOT on a child. NOT on a mannequin. NOT being held up.
- No child faces in Level 3 imagery unless explicitly approved (dignity risk too high).
- Color temperature: Warm (2700-3000K). No cool blues, no clinical white.

Absolute NOs for L3 Imagery:
- Children running, jumping, or "playing normally"
- Before/after transformation shots
- Side-by-side comparisons
- "Victory" poses or raised hands
- Smiling children on potties
- Adult diapers shown in frame (even partially)
- Urgency reds or bright yellows
- Cheerful illustrations with cartoon faces

sections.name: 2.3 Image Treatment
sections.content:
- Tone: Soft, diffused, natural light. Slight desaturation (80% of original).
- Vignette: Subtle dark edge vignette (10% opacity) to draw eye inward.
- Border: None on images. No drop shadows. Let images breathe.
- Product Priority: Product shown only in context of use, folded in a drawer, resting on a bed, placed alongside a book. Never isolated on white like a commodity.

sections.name: 2.4 CTA Styling
sections.content:
The CTA for L3 Winback E1 must be the lowest-visual-pressure call-to-action in the entire system:

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ │
│ You decide. No timeline. │
│ ═══════════════ │
│ (underline only, brand green) │
│ │
└────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

CTA Design Rules:
- NO button (no background, no border-radius, no fill)
- Text only with a single underline in `#039902`
- Font size 15px, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.06em
- Do not repeat the CTA. One CTA, one placement, once.
- No arrow, no chevron, no "→" or "›" decoration
- Hover state: subtle opacity shift (0.8), no color change, no underline animation
- Mobile: same treatment, minimum 44px tap target as a `<span>` with padding

sections.name: 2.5 Mobile Optimization
sections.content:
| Element | Desktop (600px) | Mobile (320px) |
|---------|----------------|-----------------|
| Headline | 24px | 20px |
| Body | 16px | 15px |
| Pull Quote | 18px | 16px |
| Section Padding | 32px | 24px |
| CTA | 15px | 15px, full-width tap zone |
| Logo | 140px wide | 110px wide |

- Single-column layout only
- No hover-only dependencies
- Preheader visible, first 90 characters critical in Gmail/iOS

sections.name: 2.6 Design Decisions — Traceability
sections.content:
| Decision | Rationale | Source |
|----------|-----------|--------|
| No hero image | L3 visual language must be quiet, not aspirational | 19-older-child-dignity-playbook §3.2; 15-level-messaging-matrix §4.9 |
| Cream background (`#FBF7EB`) | Warmth without clinical feel. Differentiates from competition's white/blue | 11-brand-voice-reference §14; Consolidated design skills §Brand Colors |
| Questrial 400 only | Clean, readable, non-branded feel. No bold = no visual shouting | Consolidated design skills §Typography; 11-brand-voice-reference §14 |
| No CTA button | Dignity-first design. Button implies "action expected" which is pressure | 15-level-messaging-matrix §4.4 (L3 CTA rules); GIULIANO-DEMANDS §1 L3 |
| Underline-only CTA | Least aggressive clickable treatment. Matches "permission-not-to-buy" | 2-full-email-copy-strategy §2.6 (Winback emotional arc); 19-older-child-dignity-playbook §3 |
| Wide body text spacing (1.7) | Increased white space = room to breathe. Critical for L3 emotional pacing | 15-level-messaging-matrix §4.5 (L3 tonality: spacious cadence) |
| P.S. in sign-off | Humanizes Lena. Reply invitation reinforces peer-to-peer, not brand-to-customer | 11-brand-voice-reference §1; 2-full-email-copy-strategy §Welcome E1 sign-off |
| No child faces | L3 parent dignity preserved. Product shown as tool, not identity | 19-older-child-dignity-playbook §14; 15-level-messaging-matrix §4.2 Dignity Test |
| Flat-lay product photography | Non-aspirational. Reflects real use: a pair of underwear, nothing more | 19-older-child-dignity-playbook §7 (Email Copy Example 6: flat-lay reference) |

sections.name: 2.7 Brand Standard Compliance
sections.content:
| Element | Requirement | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| From Name | "Lena from BrightKidCo" | ✅ |
| From Email | support@brightkidco.com | ✅ |
| Logo | BrightKidCo wordmark, centered | ✅ |
| Font | Questrial 400 only | ✅ |
| Primary Color | Green #039902 in palette | ✅ |
| Guarantee | 60-Day Money Back mentioned | ✅ |
| Price | 1 Pair $34.99 | ✅ |
| Lena Role | "Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons" | ✅ |
